# Grill Me

Turn a rough idea into a shared design before any implementation work starts.

## Core behavior

Interview the user relentlessly about the plan until the design is decision-complete.
Walk the design tree branch by branch and resolve dependencies in order.
If a question can be answered by exploring the codebase, inspect the repo instead of asking.

## Workflow

1. Inspect the relevant repo context first.
2. Ask one high-signal question at a time.
3. Prefer concrete options and a recommendation when tradeoffs are involved.
4. Push past vague answers until goal, scope, interfaces, constraints, edge cases, and tests are explicit.
5. Summarize locked decisions every few questions so the shared model stays aligned.
6. When the design is stable, hand off to `write-prd`.

## Rules

- Do not start implementing.
- Do not skip codebase exploration.
- Do not let "reasonable defaults" hide unresolved product decisions.
- Keep summaries and final checkpoints in Japanese unless the user asked for another language.
